name: Joe Harris
abstract: Joseph Allison Harris (1926–2016) was a big band and bebop jazz drummer. Born in Pittsburgh, on 23 December 1926, he moved to New York City in 1946 and played in the house band at the Apollo Theater before going on to play with Dizzy Gillespie.

concerts.label: Billy Eckstine, George Shearing Quintet, and The All Stars
Billy Eckstine, George Shearing Quintet, and The All Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Joseph Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play with a band called Original Royal Jazz Band. A fella called Joe Harris... He was the leader of this band... So I played quite a few jobs with him.

about_quotes.speaker: James "Sing" Miller
about_quotes.evidence: In other words, I played with Joe Harris, he's a scientist one player too. ... In other words, Joe Harris was kin to me on my grandfather's side. And I played with him about six years and didn't know it. My first cousin.

about_quotes.speaker: Father Al Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: I went down there with Octave after I left the entertainers. Then I went down there and worked with him and worked with that group... It was Octave, Joe Harris.

about_quotes.speaker: John Batiste "Bat" Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: I got with a band, with a Royal Jazz Band... Joe Harris... That was his band. Let's see, they had me... Joe Harris, he was dead. He was playing tramo.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: I had Little Jimmy Heath with me. Jimmy and Percy and Milt Jackson and Jeff Davis and Joe Harris, the drummer -- and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: I had Little Jimmy Heath with me. Jimmy and Percy and Milt Jackson and Jeff Davis and Joe Harris, the drummer -- and myself.
